q1.prompt: A 32-year-old female undergoes a cervical biopsy that confirms the presence of high-risk Human Papillomavirus (HPV) type 16. The pathogenesis of malignancy associated with this virus is driven by the viral oncoproteins E6 and E7. Which of the following best describes the mechanism of action of these oncoproteins?

options.label: Integration of the viral genome into host cell DNA
Inhibition of the p53 and Rb host proteins
Induction of chronic inflammation and cellular regeneration
Inhibition of host RNA-dependent RNA polymerase
Cleavage of precursor glycoproteins into functional subunits

q2.prompt: A 22-year-old patient presents with painful grouped vesicles on an erythematous base around the genital area, accompanied by fever and tender inguinal lymphadenopathy. The lesions later ulcerate before crusting. At which site does the causative viral agent establish latency?

q3.prompt: A 10-year-old male with a documented history of infection by Dengue virus serotype 1 (DEN-1) two years ago presents with high fever, vomiting, and a positive tourniquet test. He is subsequently diagnosed with Dengue Hemorrhagic Fever (DHF) caused by a secondary infection with serotype 2 (DEN-2). Which of the following processes best explains the pathogenesis of this severe clinical presentation?

options.label: Antigenic shift leading to the emergence of a new reassortant viral subtype
Enhanced viral entry into monocytes facilitated by heterotypic antibodies
Massive destruction of memory T cells specific to the original serotype
Viral inhibition of the host complement system to evade immune detection
Direct cytopathic effect of viral replication on the colonic epithelium

q4.prompt: A 19-year-old college student presents with a sore throat, fever, and cervical lymphadenopathy. Presuming a bacterial etiology, the clinician prescribes ampicillin. Two days later, the patient returns with a prominent, diffuse maculopapular rash. This clinical reaction to the antibiotic is a classic diagnostic clue for which underlying viral infection?

q5.prompt: A 32-year-old patient returns from travel to a tropical region with a recent history of fever, rash, and conjunctivitis. One week later, they develop progressive ascending weakness and areflexia. CSF shows elevated protein with normal cell count. This presentation suggests infection by a viral agent belonging to which family?

q6.prompt: Maraviroc is an entry inhibitor used in the management of HIV-1 infection. This drug works by binding to a specific host cell surface component, thereby preventing the virus from completing the "docking" step. What is the target of Maraviroc?

q7.prompt: A 19-year-old college student presents with fever, sore throat, and profound fatigue. Physical exam reveals posterior cervical lymphadenopathy and splenomegaly. A peripheral smear shows atypical lymphocytes. This microscopic finding is highly suggestive of an infection by a member of which viral family?

q8.prompt: Influenza A viruses undergo two types of antigenic variation that affect their epidemiology. What is the fundamental difference between antigenic "drift" and antigenic "shift"?

options.label: Drift involves segmented reassortment, while shift involves point mutations
Drift results from random point mutations, while shift results from reassortment of genomic segments
Shift occurs annually, while drift is responsible for major global pandemics
Drift only occurs in Influenza A, while shift occurs in Influenza A, B, and C
Shift involves the loss of the viral envelope, while drift involves changes to the capsid

q9.prompt: A 6-year-old child is brought to the clinic with a mild fever and a distinctive bright red rash on both cheeks, described as a "slapped cheek" appearance, with a lacy rash on the arms. Which virus is the causative agent of this condition?

q10.prompt: A 34-year-old man presents with agitation, confusion, and difficulty swallowing several weeks. He reports intense throat spasms when attempting to drink water. Examination shows hypersalivation and episodes of hydrophobia. Which of the following is the possible history of this patient?

options.label: Consumption of contaminated food or water
